platform_decisions.content: Python preferred for complex hook logic; bash for simple checks
Hook input exclusively via stdin JSON; no CLI arguments for event data
Exit code tripartite: 0=allow silently, 1=surface stderr to user (non-blocking), 2=block + surface to Claude
Blocking feedback written to stderr; stdout output ignored on exit 2
Hooks guard against irrelevant tools with early-exit pattern (check tool_name, exit 0 if not target)
Validation rules encoded as (regex, message) tuples in named list, separate from dispatch logic
Regex patterns use word boundaries and negative lookaheads to avoid false positives (e.g. grep in pipeline)
JSON parse errors exit 1 (non-blocking warning) not 2 (blocking); prevents hooks from breaking sessions
Hook config embedded as JSON docstring in hook file itself for self-documentation
Bash tool matcher is exact tool name string, not glob or regex
Strict settings: disableBypassPermissionsMode + ask for Bash + deny for WebSearch/WebFetch + allowManagedOnly
disableBypassPermissionsMode value is string 'disable' not boolean false
allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly restricts settings to MDM/managed channels only
allowManagedHooksOnly paired with allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly closes dual injection surface
sandbox.autoAllowBashIfSandboxed explicitly false; sandbox does not implicitly grant Bash rights
Sandbox network block fully specified with explicit false/empty values for all fields
Lax settings: minimum viable floor is disableBypassPermissionsMode only
MDM deployment via 4 mechanisms: managed-settings.json, macOS plist, macOS mobileconfig, Windows Registry
Windows managed settings use explicit UTF-8 encoding with ErrorActionPreference Stop
Group Policy stores entire settings JSON as single REG_SZ string (flat, not nested registry keys)
macOS mobileconfig uses PayloadScope System for machine-wide non-overridable settings
MDM templates use placeholder UUIDs requiring uuidgen replacement; verification via /status
strictKnownMarketplaces always present as explicit empty array in security configs
Tool-replacement hooks use PreToolUse exit 2 with instructional stderr message redirecting to preferred tool
Hook rules cover both direct command use and argument pattern matching (tool-name + flag combinations)
Hook main() follows guard-clause structure: parse -> validate tool -> validate command -> run rules -> emit
